name: TeVeS (Tensor-Vector-Scalar) relativistic MOND
summary: Bekenstein's relativistic completion of MOND, in which gravity is mediated by a metric, a 4-vector, and a scalar field. Reproduces MOND in the low-acceleration limit while remaining covariant and consistent with cosmological tests. Aims to replace particle dark matter with modified gravity in galaxies; passes solar-system tests by construction but struggles with cluster lensing offsets and the CMB acoustic peak structure.

addresses_puzzles.note: Reproduces flat rotation curves and Tully-Fisher relation by construction. Strongly disfavored by Bullet Cluster, CMB, and large-scale structure data; many implementations later excluded by GW170817 (graviton speed).

touches_benchmarks.note: Spatial offset between baryonic and lensing centres in the Bullet Cluster cannot be reproduced without DM; this is the canonical TeVeS-killer.
Original TeVeS predicted c_GW != c; GW170817 measurement |c_GW - c|/c < 10^-15 excludes the simplest realisations. Modified TeVeS variants compatible with c_GW = c remain niche.
Without cold collisionless DM, the height ratios and locations of CMB acoustic peaks cannot be reproduced; TeVeS does not provide the required perturbation evolution.

introduces.note: Free function shape, vector / scalar coupling, MOND scale.

history: Bekenstein (2004), 'Relativistic gravitation theory for the modified Newtonian dynamics paradigm', Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509, arXiv:astro-ph/0403694 (with erratum PRD 71, 069901). Earlier MOND was proposed by Milgrom (1983); Bekenstein-Milgrom Lagrangian formulation 1984. Bullet Cluster (Clowe et al. 2006) and GW170817 substantially constrained relativistic-MOND constructions.

references.citation: Bekenstein, 'Relativistic gravitation theory for the modified Newtonian dynamics paradigm', Phys. Rev. D 70, 083509 (2004)
